message:
fix(time+fuzz): strftime handles out-of-range tm fields without overflow (bd-7rxtm)

Added crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_strftime.rs —
a crash-detector + buffer-bound + determinism harness exercising
the pure-Rust format_strftime engine with arbitrary format bytes
and arbitrary BrokenDownTime fields.

The harness immediately found two overflow panics in
crates/frankenlibc-core/src/time/mod.rs:
  - iso_week() computed thu_yday on i32 and overflowed when the
    caller supplied extreme tm_yday/tm_wday values (%V, %G, %g
    specifiers).
  - %U and %W week-number formatting did the same (tm_yday +
    constants - tm_wday on i32).

Both sites now widen to i64 before subtraction. POSIX does not
constrain the tm field ranges at the API boundary, so defensive
widening is the correct contract — and it matches glibc, which
also never panics on out-of-range tm input.

Added format_strftime_does_not_overflow_on_extreme_tm_fields
regression test. Preserved corpus seeds from the strftime and
wordexp fuzz runs (wordexp run: 1.4M execs, clean).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
